title: Stagflation Averted, Upswing In Place
gist: Paywalled teaser: Gavekal argues the inflationary shock from the US-Iran war is already fading in the eurozone, with June HICP decelerating to 2.8% YoY (from 3.2% in May and below the 3% consensus), so stagflation risk recedes and an underlying reflationary upswing can resume.

insights.claim: Eurozone HICP inflation slowed to 2.8% year-on-year in June 2026, down from 3.2% in May, marking the first deceleration since the US-Iran war began at the end of February.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: Cooling headline inflation eases ECB tightening pressure and supports duration and eurozone risk assets.
insights.quote: Data released on Wednesday showed that eurozone HICP inflation slowed to 2.8% year-on-year in June from 3.2% in May.

insights.claim: June eurozone headline HICP of 2.8% undershot the 3% consensus among economists surveyed by Bloomberg.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: A downside surprise versus consensus signals inflation momentum is weaker than the market priced, a dovish setup for rates.
insights.quote: the headline number undershooting the 3% consensus among economists surveyed by Bloomberg

insights.claim: The inflationary shock from the US-Iran war is fading, and as it recedes the risk of eurozone economic stagflation declines, leaving room for the gentler underlying reflationary trend that preceded the war to reassert itself.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: A shift from feared stagflation to benign reflation favors cyclical and eurozone equity exposure over defensive stagflation hedges.
insights.quote: As it recedes, so does the risk of economic stagflation, leaving the eurozone free to rediscover the gentler, more benign, underlying reflationary trend that had been bedding down before the war’s start.
insights.locator: The latest batch of data from the eurozone
